zh.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Bri: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Elizabeth Nye於1607年11月出生在英國素瑞奧可利時，她的父親Oliver Nye28歲，母親Precilla28歲。她於1639年與Thomas Hurlbut在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1678年9月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年70歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Elizabeth Nye于1607年11月出生在英国素瑞奧可利时，她的父亲Oliver Nye28岁，母亲Precilla28岁。她于1639年与Thomas Hurlbut在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1678年9月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德享年70岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德維利亞 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Nye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atten eye, atten ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg ‘island’), becoming atte nye. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Nye in Winscombe, Somerset.

English: alternatively, from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atten eye, atten ee ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa ‘river’), becoming atte nee. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived near a river, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Neigh Bridge in Somerford Keynes, Wiltshire.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 聶, see   Nie   2.

variants.name: Yeo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Yong, Cheng, In, Li, Youngho, Bock, Byeong, Chi, Eunsook, Fung, Hoon, In Young.

English (Devon): from southwestern Middle English ya, yo, yeo ‘river or stream’ (Old English ēa), either a topographic name denoting residence near a stream or a habitational name from any of several minor places in Devon and Somerset named with this word. The same word is found in Nye , Rye , and Tye .
Korean: variant of Yŏ (see Yo ).

variants.name: Ney
meanings: English: variant of   Nye  .
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): Anglicized and shortened form of Gaelic Mac Niadh, see McNee .
North German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Neu  . The Jewish surname may sometimes be a shortened form of a name such as   Neuburger  .

variants.name: Nigh
meanings: English: variant of   Nye  .
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Neu   or   Ney  .

variants.name: Nie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Xiaosong, Cui, Dawei, Fei, Fen, Feng, Geng, Hui, Hui Ling, Huiming, Jin, Jing.
English (Essex and Middlesex): variant of   Nye  .

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 聶, meaning ‘whisper’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Nie (聶), the name of a fief (located in Henan province) granted to an official in the state of Wey (衛). (ii) from the placename Nie (聶), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to a son of the Duke Ding of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, reigned 1014–975  BC ).

variants.name: Rea
meanings: English: from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rye (Sussex), The Rye in High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire), Rye Hill in Epping Upland (Essex), Rye Mill in Feering (Essex), and Rye House in Stanstead Abbots (Hertfordshire). Alternatively, the name may also arise from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter eye ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa) and may also be topographic, for someone who lived near a river or stream, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rea in Hempsted (Gloucestershire), Ray Lodge in Lingfield (Surrey), and Rye Farm in Cranleigh (Surrey). Due to the similarity of Middle English forms it is now impossible to distinguish between the two explanations. See also Nye .
English: post-medieval form of   Wray  .
English: variant of   Ray  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Elizabeth Nye was born in November 1607, in Ockley, Surrey, England, her father, Oliver Nye, was 28 and her mother, Precilla, was 28. She married Thomas Hurlbut in 1639, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died in September 1678, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Elizabeth Nye est née en novembre 1607, à Ockley, Surrey, Angleterre, son père, Oliver Nye, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Precilla, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hurlbut en 1639, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en septembre 1678, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Elizabeth Nye im November 1607 in Ockley, Surrey, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Oliver Nye 28 und ihre Mutter Precilla 28. Sie hat 1639 in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Thomas Hurlbut geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im September 1678 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Elizabeth Nye è nata il nel novembre 1607 nel Ockley, Surrey, England, sua padre, Oliver Nye, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Precilla, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hurlbut nel 1639, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Elizabeth Nyeが1607年11月にイギリススリーオックリーで生まれた時，父Oliver Nyeは28歳，母Precillaは28歳であった。1639年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドでThomas Hurlbutと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1678年9月にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドで70歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドヴィラッジ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.Wethersfield Village Cemetery, Wethersfi: 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드 비라지 세멧리
ko.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Elizabeth Nye(이)가 1607년 11월 영국 수리 옥클리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Oliver Nye(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Precilla(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1639년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 Thomas Hurlbut(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1678년 9월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드 비라지 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Elizabeth Nye nasceu em novembro de 1607, em Ockley, Surrey, Inglaterra, seu pai, Oliver Nye, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Precilla, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Hurlbut em 1639, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em setembro de 1678, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Elizabeth Nye родилась ноябрь 1607 г. в местности оккли, сурри, Англия, её отцу по имени Oliver Nye было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Precilla, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hurlbut в 1639 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла сентябрь 1678 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности вилладж кеметири, ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sarah Elizabeth Nye nació en noviembre de 1607, en Ockley, Surrey, Inglaterra, su papá, Oliver Nye, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Precilla, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Thomas Hurlbut en 1639, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en septiembre de 1678, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Village Cemetery, Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
